en: Hear, O sons, a father's instruction, and be attentive, that you may gain insight,
zh: 孩子們，要聽父親的教訓， 留心學習哲理；

en: for I give you good precepts; do not forsake my teaching.
zh: 因為我授予你們的，是美好的學問， 我的訓誨，你們不可離棄。

en: When I was a son with my father, tender, the only one in the sight of my mother,
zh: 我在我父親面前還是小孩子， 在我母親面前是獨一的嬌兒的時候，

en: he taught me and said to me, "Let your heart hold fast my words; keep my commandments, and live.
zh: 父親教導我，對我說： “你的心要持守我的話， 你要遵守我的誡命，就可以存活；

en: Get wisdom; get insight; do not forget, and do not turn away from the words of my mouth.
zh: 要求取智慧和哲理， 不可忘記，也不可偏離我口中的話。

en: Do not forsake her, and she will keep you; love her, and she will guard you.
zh: 不可離棄智慧，智慧就必護衛你； 喜愛智慧，智慧就必看顧你。

en: The beginning of wisdom is this: Get wisdom, and whatever you get, get insight.
zh: 智慧的開端 是求取智慧， 要用你所得的一切換取哲理。

en: Prize her highly, and she will exalt you; she will honor you if you embrace her.
zh: 你要高舉智慧，智慧就必使你高升； 你要懷抱智慧，智慧就必使你得尊榮。

en: She will place on your head a graceful garland; she will bestow on you a beautiful crown."
zh: 智慧必把華冠加在你頭上， 把榮冕賜給你。”

en: Hear, my son, and accept my words, that the years of your life may be many.
zh: 我兒，你要聽，並要接受我所說的， 這樣，你就必延年益壽。

en: I have taught you the way of wisdom; I have led you in the paths of uprightness.
zh: 我指教你走智慧的道， 引導你行正直的路。

en: When you walk, your step will not be hampered, and if you run, you will not stumble.
zh: 你行走的時候，腳步必不會受阻礙； 你奔跑的時候，也不會跌倒。

en: Keep hold of instruction; do not let go; guard her, for she is your life.
zh: 你要堅守教訓，不可放鬆； 要謹守教訓，因為那是你的生命。

en: Do not enter the path of the wicked, and do not walk in the way of the evil.
zh: 不可走進惡人的路徑， 不可踏上壞人的道路。

en: Avoid it; do not go on it; turn away from it and pass on.
zh: 要躲避，不可從那裡經過， 要轉身離去。

en: For they cannot sleep unless they have done wrong; they are robbed of sleep unless they have made someone stumble.
zh: 因為他們不行惡，就不能入睡； 不使人跌倒，就要失眠。

en: For they eat the bread of wickedness and drink the wine of violence.
zh: 他們吃的，是奸惡的飯； 他們喝的，是強暴的酒。

en: But the path of the righteous is like the light of dawn, which shines brighter and brighter until full day.
zh: 義人的路徑卻像黎明的曙光， 越來越明亮，直到日午。

en: The way of the wicked is like deep darkness; they do not know over what they stumble.
zh: 惡人的道路幽暗， 他們不知道自己因甚麼跌倒。

en: My son, be attentive to my words; incline your ear to my sayings.
zh: 我兒，要留心聽我的話， 側耳聽我所說的。

en: Let them not escape from your sight; keep them within your heart.
zh: 不可讓它們離開你的眼目， 要謹記在你的心中。

en: For they are life to those who find them, and healing to all their flesh.
zh: 因為得著它們就是得著生命， 整個人也得著醫治。

en: Keep your heart with all vigilance, for from it flow the springs of life.
zh: 你要謹守你的心，勝過謹守一切， 因為生命的泉源由此而出。

en: Put away from you crooked speech, and put devious talk far from you.
zh: 你要除掉欺詐的口， 遠離乖謬的嘴唇。

en: Let your eyes look directly forward, and your gaze be straight before you.
zh: 你雙眼要向前正視， 你的眼睛要向前直望。

en: Ponder the path of your feet; then all your ways will be sure.
zh: 你要謹慎你腳下的路徑， 你一切所行的就必穩妥。

en: Do not swerve to the right or to the left; turn your foot away from evil.
zh: 不可偏左偏右， 要使你的腳遠離惡事。
